graph.name: American Family Group - Main Street America Legacy Application Modernization
graph.notes: American Family Group is undergoing a modernization and transformation of a legacy application for its Main Street America independent agents. The architecture represents a hybrid state where new features, significant enhancements, and major bug fixes are implemented in a serverless AWS stack, while existing 'user management' and other functionalities still reside on on-premise Tomcat servers. The strategy is to 'slowly strangle' the legacy application by progressively migrating its functions to the cloud. This modernization has led to significant benefits including the shutdown of 25 on-prem servers, a 25% increase in user satisfaction, and a 10% increase in new users.

nodes.name: Independent Agents
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Independent agents accessing the single-page application (SPA).

nodes.name: CDN for SPA
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Serves the single-page application (SPA) content. Modernized to deliver content previously served by on-prem Apache servers.

nodes.name: SPA Hosting
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores the static assets for the single-page application (SPA). WORKLOAD_PEEK: Modernized to host the application content, replacing on-prem Apache.

nodes.name: Backend API Gateway
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Entry point for all backend API calls from the SPA. Replaces on-prem load balanced Tomcat servers for API routing.

nodes.name: Lambda Authorizer
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: A modern Lambda authorizer used by API Gateway to validate user access and ensure proper authorization for API calls.

nodes.name: Backend Logic Lambda
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: A serverless Python Lambda function that implements the modernized application logic. It's designed to scale up and down easily and handles new features, significant enhancements, and bug fixes being migrated from the legacy system.

nodes.name: User Information Cache
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores and caches user information. Designed as a regional cache to be accessible by Lambda instances across multiple Availability Zones, ensuring consistency and high availability even if an AZ goes down. WORKLOAD_PEEK: Used by the main Lambda function to store and retrieve user session data.

nodes.name: Legacy Tomcat Application
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Represents the on-premise load balanced Tomcat servers running Java script. Still hosts some legacy functionality, such as user management. It is being gradually 'strangled' as functions are migrated to the serverless AWS stack. The icon in the diagram uses the AWS EC2 symbol to represent this compute environment despite being on-prem.
